items.title: Sex- and etiology-specific effects on predictive processing in the inferior colliculus of two rat models of autism
items.author: Manuel S. Malmierca
items.content: This study examined auditory predictive processing at the single-neuron level in the inferior colliculus of genetic and environmental rat models of autism. Authors identified sex- and etiology-dependent alterations across auditory midbrain divisions, revealing early differences in sensory computations.

items.title: Auditory cortex modulates call duration in rats
items.author: Michael Brecht
items.content: Neuronal and pharmacological analysis of rat auditory cortex reveals its direct role in regulating vocal production, identifying distinct neuron classes that predict call timing and duration and demonstrating auditory modulation of vocal behavior

items.title: LukS-PV targeting C5aR inhibits EMT in hepatocellular carcinoma via the BCL6/HDAC6/HSPD1 axis
items.author: Shanshan Zhang
items.content: LukS-PV targets C5aR to inhibit HCC EMT and metastasis via the BCL6/HDAC6/HSPD1 axis, promoting HSPD1 acetylation and ubiquitin-mediated degradation, highlighting its potential as a targeted HCC therapeutic agent.

items.title: Notch3 regulates pericyte phenotypic plasticity in colorectal cancer
items.author: Vasiliki Koliaraki
items.content: Single-cell RNA sequencing, lineage tracing, and in vivo colorectal cancer studies reveal the role of Notch3 signaling in pericyte phenotype switching and its effect on blood vessel integrity.

items.title: Structural analysis reveals that water molecules mediate self-activation of GPR99
items.author: Heng Liu
items.content: GPR99 self-activation is promoted by the second extracellular loop (ECL2) occupying the orthosteric pocket, which is stabilized by a water-mediated polar network. These findings provide a structural basis for this receptor’s physiological roles and a theoretical framework for designing drugs targeting the receptor.

items.title: Transient proliferation by reversible YAP and mitogen control of the cyclin D1/p27 ratio
items.author: Tobias Meyer
items.content: Quantitative image-based cytometry and spatial analysis of proliferation reveal how multiple signalling pathways integrate over time to coordinate both cell cycle entry and exit in epithelial monolayers.

items.title: Co-variation and trade-offs in ontogenetic scaling of growth and metabolic rates in teleost fish
items.author: Tommy Norin
items.content: Analysis of metabolic scaling in teleost fish shows that ontogenetic scaling of growth correlates positively with maintenance metabolic demands but negatively with metabolic scope. These results help explain variation in metabolic scaling and energetic trade-offs.

items.title: PiR48444 inhibits MSC osteogenic differentiation and bone regeneration via targeting METTL7A/eIF4E-mediated BMP2 m6A methylation
items.author: Lihong Wu
items.content: piR48444 targets METTL7A to inhibit BMP2 mRNA m6A methylation and METTL7A/eIF4E complex-mediated BMP2 mRNA stability and translation, ultimately inhibiting osteogenic differentiation of MSCs and bone regeneration.
